ground_truth.description: Photo of famous landmark Royal Exhibition Building in Melbourne. Somewhat covered by trees, could be challenging without metadata.

reverse_geocoded.formatted_address: Carlton Gardens/Victoria St, Carlton VIC 3053, Australia

details.description: A long, tree-lined path in a formal city park leads to an ornate white nineteenth‑century exhibition hall. The deciduous plane trees arch overhead with brown autumn foliage, framing a symmetrical lawn and an elaborate multi‑tiered fountain in the center. Beyond the fountain rises a grand façade with a huge semicircular fan/rose window set in a recessed arch, arched doorways, pilasters and classical detailing. Soft late‑afternoon light and a few pedestrians emphasize the tranquil setting. This is the south approach to the Royal Exhibition Building within Carlton Gardens in Melbourne.

entities.name: Royal Exhibition Building
entities.notes: Architect Joseph Reed (Reed & Barnes); completed 1879–1880 for the Melbourne International Exhibition; UNESCO World Heritage site (with Carlton Gardens).

entities.name: Royal Exhibition Building
entities.notes: Iconic Melbourne exhibition hall; Renaissance Revival/Italianate influences.

entities.name: Hochgurtel Fountain
entities.notes: Elaborate tiered exhibition fountain designed by Josef Hochgurtel for the 1880 Melbourne International Exhibition.

entities.name: Carlton Gardens
entities.notes: Formal Victorian-era gardens surrounding the Royal Exhibition Building; part of a UNESCO World Heritage property.

entities.name: Renaissance Revival (Victorian) architecture
entities.notes: The building’s composition blends Renaissance Revival with Italianate and Byzantine/Romanesque influences—arched portal, rose window, classical ornament.

entities.notes: Inscribed 2004 for significance as a surviving Great Exhibition ensemble.

details.description: A tranquil park scene in Carlton Gardens, Melbourne. A broad paved path runs along the left, shaded by tall, bare-branched trees whose autumn foliage forms a warm golden canopy. To the right lies a well-kept grassy lawn. At the far end of the axis sits a white neoclassical building with a symmetrical facade and a prominent circular fanlight/arced window above a central entrance. In front of the building, a multi-tiered fountain forms a focal point, with a few people visible near the steps. The overall mood is calm and formal, with the seasonal light giving the pale facade a soft glow.

details.description: A wide pedestrian path in a formal park lined with mature plane trees leads toward a grand historic exhibition hall. Through the autumn foliage, the pale facade shows tall arched openings and a large fanlight beneath the central dome (partly visible). In the forecourt stands a large multi-tiered white fountain. People stroll along the path on a clear late-afternoon day. This is the south approach to the Royal Exhibition Building in Carlton Gardens, Melbourne.

details.description: A straight, tree-lined pedestrian avenue with tall, bare-branched trees forms a natural tunnel toward a white neoclassical building with arched windows and a central facade. In front of the building sits a multi-tiered white fountain. The scene is set on a green lawn with scattered fallen leaves and a few people walking along the path. The overall setting resembles Carlton Gardens in Melbourne, Australia, with a view toward the Royal Exhibition Building in the distance.

details.description: A scenic view looking down a tree-lined avenue within the Carlton Gardens in Melbourne. Towering London Plane trees with autumn foliage in shades of brown and orange frame the composition, their branches arching over a paved pathway on the left and a green lawn on the right. In the background stands the historic Royal Exhibition Building, a grand white structure featuring a large arched entrance and classical architectural details. Directly in front of the building's southern entrance is the ornate Hochgurtel Fountain, a large Victorian-era fountain with water spraying from its tiers. The scene is illuminated by warm sunlight, casting long shadows and highlighting the texture of the tree bark and leaves.

details.description: A vertical shot looking down a tree-lined path towards the Royal Exhibition Building in Melbourne. The path is paved and bordered by a lush green lawn on the right. Large, mature London Plane trees with brown autumnal leaves frame the view, creating a natural tunnel effect. In the mid-ground, the ornate, multi-tiered Hochgurtel Fountain stands in front of the building. The Royal Exhibition Building itself is a grand, white structure featuring a large central dome and a prominent arched window on its southern facade. The scene is captured in soft, late-afternoon light, casting a warm glow on the upper branches and the building's dome.

details.description: A long, tree-lined avenue in a formal garden leads to a large white historic exhibition hall. The facade features a high semicircular fanlight window above arched doorways, pilasters and classical detailing. Centered in front of the building is a grand multi-tiered sculptural fountain with allegorical figures. The scene is in late-afternoon light with autumn foliage, open lawns and a paved path. This is the south approach to the Royal Exhibition Building in Carlton Gardens, Melbourne.

details.description: A formal urban plaza framed by a row of tall, leafless deciduous trees on the left, forming a tunnel-like pathway toward a white neoclassical building in the background. In front of the building sits a multi-tier stone fountain with sculptural details, situated on a manicured lawn and surrounded by low-lying shrubs. The building features a symmetrical façade with large arched windows on the ground level and a prominent circular/rose window above the central entrance. The scene is bathed in warm, low-angle light typical of late afternoon in autumn, with a few pedestrians visible near the fountain and along the path.

In the foreground is a well-kept green lawn and a central multi-tier fountain. Bare deciduous trees arch overhead, their branches forming a natural canopy with hints of autumnal orange leaves. The white facade of the building features arches and large windows, creating a dramatic backdrop as a few people walk toward the structure.

details.description: A vertical photograph looking down a tree-lined avenue, likely the Grand Allée, in Carlton Gardens, Melbourne. The image captures a paved asphalt path on the left and a green lawn on the right, flanked by tall, mature deciduous trees (likely London Plane trees) with autumn foliage in shades of brown and orange. These trees frame a distant view of the Royal Exhibition Building, a grand white structure featuring Beaux-Arts architecture, a large arched fanlight window, and a central dome. Directly in front of the building's southern entrance stands the ornate, multi-tiered Hochgurtel Fountain. Small figures of pedestrians can be seen walking along the path and near the fountain.

details.description: A view looking north along a paved pedestrian path in Carlton Gardens towards the southern facade of the Royal Exhibition Building in Melbourne. The scene is framed by tall, mature plane trees with brown, autumnal leaves. In the center foreground, before the building, is the ornate, multi-tiered white Hochgurtel Fountain. The Royal Exhibition Building features a grand white facade with a large arched fanlight window and classical detailing. The lighting suggests late afternoon, with warm light hitting the upper parts of the trees and the building's dome, which is partially obscured by the canopy. Several people are visible walking on the paths and lawn near the fountain.
